Kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ mũtũmwo Paũlũ, na rĩrĩ, ndirĩ mũtũmwo ũtũmĩtwo nĩ andũ o na kana ngatũmwo na njĩra ya mũndũ, no ndũmĩtwo nĩ Jesũ Kristũ na Ngai Ithe witũ, ũrĩa wamũriũkirie kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ.
English: Paul, an apostle—not from men, nor through man, but through Jesus Christ,† and God the Father, who raised him from the dead—

Kikuyu: Niĩ hamwe na ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ othe arĩa tũrĩ nao, nĩ ithuĩ tũramũtũmĩra marũa maya, Kũrĩ inyuĩ andũ a makanitha ma Galatia:
English: and all the brothers‡ who are with me, to the assemblies of Galatia:

Kikuyu: Mũrogĩa na wega na thayũ iria ciumĩte kũrĩ Ngai Ithe witũ, o na kũrĩ Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ,
English: Grace to you and peace from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ,

Kikuyu: ũrĩa werutire nĩ ũndũ wa mehia maitũ nĩguo atũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ mahinda maya tũrĩ, marĩa maiyũrĩtwo nĩ maũndũ mooru, kũringana na wendi wa Ngai Ithe witũ,
English: who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us out of this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father—

Kikuyu: nake arotũũra akumagio nginya tene na tene. Ameni.
English: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩkũgega ndĩragega nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa mũrahiũha gũtigana na ũrĩa wamwĩtire na ũndũ wa wega wa Kristũ, mũkagarũrũkĩra ũhoro mwega wa mũthemba ũngĩ,
English: I marvel that you are so quickly deserting him who called you in the grace of Christ to a different “good news”,

Kikuyu: naguo ti Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega o na atĩa. No nĩ kũrĩ andũ maramũhĩngĩĩca makĩenda gũthũkia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Kristũ.
English: but there isn’t another “good news.” Only there are some who trouble you and want to pervert the Good News of Christ.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, o na kũngĩkorwo nĩ ithuĩ o na kana mũraika uumĩte igũrũ, ũngĩmũhunjĩria ũhoro mwega ũngĩ tiga ũrĩa twamũhunjĩirie-rĩ, mũndũ ũcio aronyiitwo nĩ kĩrumi nginya tene!
English: But even though we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you any “good news” other than that which we preached to you, let him be cursed.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa tũmwĩrĩte, o na rĩu nĩ nguuga o rĩngĩ atĩrĩ, mũndũ o wothe angĩmũhunjĩria ũhoro mwega ũngĩ tiga ũrĩa mwetĩkirie-rĩ, aronyiitwo nĩ kĩrumi nginya tene!
English: As we have said before, so I now say again: if any man preaches to you any “good news” other than that which you received, let him be cursed.

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ngiuga ũguo-rĩ, kaĩ arĩ andũ ndĩrenda kwĩyendithia kũrĩ o, kana nĩ kũrĩ Ngai? Kana hihi nĩ andũ ndĩrenda gũkenia? Korwo nĩ andũ ngeragia gũkenia-rĩ, ndingĩkorwo ndĩ ndungata ya Kristũ.
English: For am I now seeking the favor of men, or of God? Or am I striving to please men? For if I were still pleasing men, I wouldn’t be a servant of Christ.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ngwenda mũmenye atĩ Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega ndamũhunjĩirie ti ũhoro woimanĩte na mũndũ.
English: But I make known to you, brothers, concerning the Good News which was preached by me, that it is not according to man.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio ndiaheirwo nĩ mũndũ, o na kana ngĩthomithio nĩ mũndũ; no nĩ kũguũrĩrio ndaguũrĩirio Ũhoro ũcio nĩ Jesũ Kristũ.
English: For I didn’t receive it from man, nor was I taught it, but it came to me through revelation of Jesus Christ.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩmũiguĩte ũhoro wa mĩthiĩre yakwa ya tene, ũrĩa ndaatũire njĩkaga ndĩ thĩinĩ wa ndini ya Ayahudi, na ũrĩa ndanyariiraga kanitha wa Ngai na hinya ngĩgeria kũũniina.
English: For you have heard of my way of living in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the assembly of God and ravaged it.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ha ũhoro wa maũndũ makoniĩ ndini ya Ayahudi, nĩ ndaathiĩte mbere o na gũkĩra Ayahudi aingĩ a riika rĩakwa na ndaarĩ na kĩyo kĩnene gĩa kũrũmia mĩtugo ya maithe maitũ.
English: I advanced in the Jews’ religion beyond many of my own age among my countrymen, being more exceedingly zealous for the traditions of my fathers.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Ngai ũrĩa wanyamũrire kuuma ndaciarwo, na akĩnjĩta nĩ ũndũ wa wega wake-rĩ, nĩ oonire arĩ wega,
English: But when it was the good pleasure of God, who separated me from my mother’s womb and called me through his grace,

Kikuyu: anguũrĩrie Mũriũ nĩgeetha hunjagĩrie andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ ũhoro wake; gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ndaahoire kĩrĩra,
English: to reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the Gentiles, I didn’t immediately confer with flesh and blood,

Kikuyu: o na ndiambatire Jerusalemu kuonana na arĩa maarĩ atũmwo mbere yakwa, no ndathiire bũrũri wa Arabia na ihenya, na thuutha ũcio ngĩcooka Dameski.
English: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me, but I went away into Arabia. Then I returned to Damascus.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, thuutha wa mĩaka ĩtatũ, nĩndambatire Jerusalemu nĩguo tũkamenyane na Petero, na ngĩikara nake matukũ ikũmi na matano.
English: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Peter, and stayed with him fifteen days.

Kikuyu: Tũtionanire na atũmwo acio angĩ, tiga o Jakubu, mũrũ wa nyina na Mwathani.
English: But of the other apostles I saw no one except James, the Lord’s brother.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro ũyũ ndĩramwandĩkĩra, ndĩramwĩra ndĩ mbere ya Ngai atĩ nĩ wa ma na ndiraheenania.
English: Now about the things which I write to you, behold,§ before God, I’m not lying.

Kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio nĩndacookire ngĩthiĩ Suriata na Kilikia.
English: Then I came to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.

Kikuyu: Niĩ mwene ndiamenyekete nĩ makanitha ma Judea marĩa marĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ.
English: I was still unknown by face to the assemblies of Judea which were in Christ,

Kikuyu: No kũiguamaiguagaatĩrĩ, “Mũndũũrĩa ũratũire atũnyariiraga rĩu nĩwe ũrahunjia wĩtĩkio ũrĩa aageragia kũniina mbere ĩyo.”
English: but they only heard, “He who once persecuted us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩgooca Ngai nĩ ũndũ wakwa.
English: So they glorified God in me.

Kikuyu: Mĩaka ikũmi na ĩna yathira nĩndambatire rĩngĩ Jerusalemu, na ihinda rĩĩrĩ tũgĩthiĩ hamwe na Baranaba o na tũgĩthiĩ na Tito.
English: Then after a period of fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus also with me.

Kikuyu: Ndathiire tondũ Ngai nĩanguũrĩirie thiĩ kuo, na nĩguo ngataarĩrie atũmwo Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega ũrĩa hunjagĩria andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ. No ndataarĩirie o arĩa monekaga marĩ atongoria hatarĩ andũ angĩ, nĩgeetha gũtigatuĩke atĩ ihenya rĩrĩa ndatengʼeraga, o na kana rĩrĩa ndatengʼerete, rĩarĩ rĩa tũhũ.
English: I went up by revelation, and I laid before them the Good News which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately before those who were respected, for fear that I might be running, or had run, in vain.

Kikuyu: No o na Tito, ũrĩa warĩ hamwe na niĩ, ndaringĩrĩirio kũrua, o na gũtuĩka aarĩ Mũyunani.
English: But not even Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro ũyũ wa kũrua wagĩire ho tondũ nĩ kwarĩ na ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ a maheeni arĩa maatoonyete gatagatĩ gaitũ na hitho marĩ athigaani nĩguo matuĩrie ũhoro wa wĩyathi ũrĩa tũkoragwo naguo tũrĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ, makĩenda gũtũtua ngombo.
English: This was because of the false brothers secretly brought in, who stole in to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage,

Kikuyu: Tũtietĩkĩrire ũhoro wao o na hanini, nĩgeetha ma ya Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega ĩtũũre na inyuĩ.
English: to whom we gave no place in the way of subjection, not for an hour, that the truth of the Good News might continue with you.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ arĩa moonekaga taarĩ o anene-rĩ, (harĩ niĩ hatiarĩ na ũtiganu o ũrĩa mangĩakorirwo marĩ, nĩgũkorwo Ngai ndaroraga mũndũ ũrĩa ahaana) andũ acio gũtirĩ ũndũ moongereire harĩ ndũmĩrĩri yakwa.
English: But from those who were reputed to be important—whatever they were, it makes no difference to me; God doesn’t show partiality to man—they, I say, who were respected imparted nothing to me,

Kikuyu: O na rĩrĩ, o nĩmoonire atĩ niĩ nĩndehokeirwo wĩra wa kũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, o ta ũrĩa Petero eehokeirwo wĩra wa kũhunjĩria Ayahudi.
English: but to the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the Good News for the uncircumcised, even as Peter with the Good News for the circumcised—

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ngai ũrĩa warutaga wĩra thĩinĩ wa ũtungata wa Petero arĩ mũtũmwo kũrĩ Ayahudi, nowe warutaga wĩra ũtungata-inĩ wakwa ndĩ mũtũmwo kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ.
English: for he who worked through Peter in the apostleship with the circumcised also worked through me with the Gentiles—

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jakubu, na Petero, na Johana, arĩa moonagwo marĩ itugĩ cia gũtiira kanitha-rĩ, nĩmatwamũkĩrire tũrĩ hamwe na Baranaba na guoko kwa ũrĩo kwa ngwatanĩro, rĩrĩa maamenyire wega wa Ngai ũrĩa ndaaheetwo. Nĩmetĩkĩrire atĩ ithuĩ tũthiĩ tũkahunjĩrie andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, nao mathiĩ kũrĩ Ayahudi.
English: and when they perceived the grace that was given to me, James and Cephas and John, those who were reputed to be pillars, gave to Barnabas and me the right hand of fellowship, that we should go to the Gentiles, and they to the circumcision.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũrĩa maatwĩrire twĩkage nĩ atĩ tũririkanage athĩĩni na noguo ũndũ ũrĩa niĩ mwene ndaarĩ na kĩyo gĩa gwĩka.
English: They only asked us to remember the poor—which very thing I was also zealous to do.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Petero ookire Antiokia,* nĩndamũkararirie mbere ya andũ tondũ nĩahĩtĩtie mũno.
English: But when Peter came to Antioch, I resisted him to his face, because he stood condemned.

Kikuyu: Andũ amwe moimĩte kũrĩ Jakubu matanakinya-rĩ, Petero nĩarĩĩanagĩra na andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, no rĩrĩa maakinyire, akĩmeherera, na akĩĩamũrania na andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ tondũ nĩetigagĩra andũ arĩa maarĩ a gĩkundi kĩa andũ arĩa maruaga.
English: For before some people came from James, he ate with the Gentiles. But when they came, he drew back and separated himself, fearing those who were of the circumcision.

Kikuyu: O na Ayahudi arĩa angĩ makĩnyiitanĩra na Petero ũhinga- inĩ wake, o nginya o na Baranaba akĩhĩtithio nĩ ũhinga ũcio wao.
English: And the rest of the Jews joined him in his hypocrisy, so that even Barnabas was carried away with their hypocrisy.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndonire atĩ matiekaga kũringana na ma ya Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega, ngĩĩra Petero atĩrĩ, andũ othe makĩiguaga, “Angĩkorwo wee ũrĩ Mũyahudi, ũtũũraga na mĩtugo ya andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ na ti ta Mũyahudi-rĩ, ũngĩkĩhota atĩa kũhatĩrĩria andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ atĩ nĩguo marũmĩrĩre mĩtugo ya Ayahudi?
English: But when I saw that they didn’t walk uprightly according to the truth of the Good News, I said to Peter before them all, “If you, being a Jew, live as the Gentiles do, and not as the Jews do, why do you compel the Gentiles to live as the Jews do?

Kikuyu: “Ithuĩ arĩa tũciarĩtwo tũrĩ Ayahudi na tũtarĩ ehia ta andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ-rĩ,
English: “We, being Jews by nature and not Gentile sinners,

Kikuyu: nĩtũũĩ atĩ mũndũ ndatuĩkaga mũthingu nĩ ũndũ wa gwathĩkĩra watho, no atuĩkaga mũthingu nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia Jesũ Kristũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio o na ithuĩ twĩtĩkĩtie Kristũ Jesũ nĩguo tũtuuo athingu nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia Kristũ, no ti ũndũ wa gwathĩkĩra watho, tondũ gũtirĩ mũndũ ũgaatuuo mũthingu nĩ ũndũ wa gwathĩkĩra watho.
English: yet knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ, even we believed in Christ Jesus, that we might be justified by faith in Christ and not by the works of the law, because no flesh will be justified by the works of the law.

Kikuyu: “Angĩgĩkorwo rĩrĩa tũgũcaria gũtuuo athingu nĩ Kristũ kuonanagia atĩ ithuĩ ene tũrĩ ehia-rĩ, ũguo nĩ kuuga atĩ Kristũ atungatagĩra mehia? Hĩ! Kũroaga gũtuĩka ũguo!
English: But if while we sought to be justified in Christ, we ourselves also were found sinners, is Christ a servant of sin? Certainly not!

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo nĩ ngwaka rĩngĩ kĩrĩa ndaanangire-rĩ, nĩkuonania atĩ niĩ nĩnyunĩte watho.
English: For if I build up again those things which I destroyed, I prove myself a law-breaker.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo na ũndũ wa watho nĩndakuĩrĩire watho ũcio nĩguo ndũũre muoyo ndĩ wa Ngai.
English: For I through the law died to the law, that I might live to God.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩndĩkĩtie kwambanĩrio hamwe na Kristũ, na rĩu ti niĩ ndũũraga muoyo, no Kristũ nĩwe ũtũũraga muoyo thĩinĩ wakwa. Muoyo ũrĩa ndũũraga mwĩrĩ-inĩ ũyũ ndũũraga na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia Mũrũ wa Ngai, ũrĩa wanyendire na akĩĩruta nĩ ũndũ wakwa.
English: I have been crucified with Christ, and it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me. That life which I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself up for me.

Kikuyu: Ndingĩĩtĩkĩra gũtua wega wa Ngai kĩndũ gĩa tũhũ, tondũ kũngĩtuĩka atĩ ũthingu wonekaga nĩ ũndũ wa watho-rĩ, Kristũ aakuire tũhũ!”
English: I don’t reject the grace of God. For if righteousness is through the law, then Christ died for nothing!”

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ inyuĩ Agalatia aya akĩĩgu! Mũrogetwo nũũ? O inyuĩ muonirio wega Jesũ Kristũ arĩ mwambe mũtĩ igũrũ.
English: Foolish Galatians, who has bewitched you not to obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was openly portrayed among you as crucified?

Kikuyu: No nyende kũmenya ũndũ ũmwe tu kuuma kũrĩ inyuĩ: Mwaheirwo Roho nĩ ũndũ wa gwathĩkĩra watho, kana nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia ũhoro ũrĩa mwaiguire?
English: I just want to learn this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ mũrĩ akĩĩgu atĩa? Mwambĩrĩirie na Roho wa Ngai, na rĩu mũrenda kũrĩkĩrĩria na hinya wa mwĩrĩ?
English: Are you so foolish? Having begun in the Spirit, are you now completed in the flesh?

Kikuyu: Anga mwathĩĩnirio na maũndũ maingĩ ũguo o tũhũ, angĩkorwo ũhoro ũcio wothe warĩ wa tũhũ?
English: Did you suffer so many things in vain, if it is indeed in vain?

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai amũheaga Roho wake na akaringaga ciama gatagatĩ kanyu nĩ ũndũ wanyu gwathĩkĩra watho, kana nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia ũhoro ũrĩa mwaiguire?
English: He therefore who supplies the Spirit to you and does miracles among you, does he do it by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

Kikuyu: Ta ririkanai ũhoro wa Iburahĩmu, Maandĩko moigĩte atĩrĩ: “Etĩkirie Ngai, nake Ngai akĩmũtua mũthingu.”
English: Even so, Abraham “believed God, and it was counted to him for righteousness.”*

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio kĩmenyei atĩ andũ arĩa metĩkĩtie nĩo ciana cia Iburahĩmu.
English: Know therefore that those who are of faith are children of Abraham.

Kikuyu: Na tondũ Maandĩko marĩa matheru nĩmooigĩte o mbere atĩ Ngai nĩagatua andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ athingu na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia-rĩ, na Maandĩko macio nĩmahunjĩirie Iburahĩmu Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega o kabere, makiuga atĩrĩ: “Ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩikaarathimwo nĩ ũndũ waku.”
English: The Scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, preached the Good News beforehand to Abraham, saying, “In you all the nations will be blessed.”*

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ arĩa metĩkĩtie nĩmarathimanagĩrio hamwe na Iburahĩmu, mũndũ ũcio warĩ mwĩtĩkia.
English: So then, those who are of faith are blessed with the faithful Abraham.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe mehokaga ũhoro wa gwathĩkĩra watho-rĩ, marĩ na kĩrumi, nĩgũkorwo Maandĩko moigaga atĩrĩ, “Kũgwatwo nĩ kĩrumi nĩ mũndũ o wothe ũtahingagia maũndũ marĩa mothe mandĩkĩtwo Ibuku-inĩ rĩa Watho.”
English: For as many as are of the works of the law are under a curse. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who doesn’t continue in all things that are written in the book of the law, to do them.”*

Kikuyu: Nĩtũũĩ wega atĩ gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩtuuo mũthingu mbere ya Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa gwathĩkĩra watho, tondũ Maandĩko moigĩte atĩrĩ: “Mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu agaatũũra muoyo nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia.”
English: Now that no man is justified by the law before God is evident, for, “The righteous will live by faith.”*

Kikuyu: No watho-rĩ, nduumanĩte na ũhoro wa gwĩtĩkia; handũ ha ũguo Maandĩko moigaga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũrĩa wathĩkagĩra maũndũ ma watho agaatũũrio muoyo nĩ mo.”
English: The law is not of faith, but, “The man who does them will live by them.”*

Kikuyu: Nake Kristũ nĩatũkũũrire agĩtũruta kĩrumi-inĩ kĩa watho na ũndũ wake mwene gũtuĩka kĩrumi ithenya riitũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Kũgwatwo nĩ kĩrumi nĩ mũndũ o wothe wambĩtwo mũtĩ-igũrũ.”
English: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law, having become a curse for us. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree,”*

Kikuyu: Aatũkũũrire nĩguo kĩrathimo kĩrĩa Iburahĩmu aarathimĩtwo nakĩo gĩkinyĩre andũ-a- Ndũrĩrĩ na ũndũ wa Kristũ Jesũ, nĩguo atĩ na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia twamũkĩre Roho ũrĩa weranĩirwo.
English: that the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, rekei ndĩmuonie na ngerekano yumanĩte na maũndũ marĩa mũũĩ. O ta ũrĩa gũtarĩ mũndũ ũngĩhota kweheria ũndũ kana kuongerera ũndũ harĩ kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa gĩthondeketwo nĩ mũndũ-rĩ, no taguo ũhoro ũyũ ũtariĩ.
English: Brothers, speaking of human terms, though it is only a man’s covenant, yet when it has been confirmed, no one makes it void or adds to it.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ marĩa meranĩirwo, meerĩirwo Iburahĩmu na mbeũ yake. Maandĩko matiugĩte “mbeũ ciake”, taarĩ ũhoro wa andũ aingĩ, no moigĩte “na mbeũ yake”, ũguo nĩ kuuga mũndũ ũmwe, na nĩwe Kristũ.
English: Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his offspring.† He doesn’t say, “To descendants‡”, as of many, but as of one, “To your offspring”,* which is Christ.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa ndĩrenda kuuga nĩ atĩrĩ: Watho, ũcio wokire thuutha wa mĩaka 430, ndũngĩeheria kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa gĩekĩrĩtwo ho nĩ Ngai mbere ĩyo, na ndũngĩtũma kĩĩranĩro kĩu gĩtuĩke gĩa tũhũ.
English: Now I say this: A covenant confirmed beforehand by God in Christ, the law, which came four hundred thirty years after, does not annul, so as to make the promise of no effect.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, angĩkorwo igai rĩngĩoneka nĩ ũndũ wa watho-rĩ, rĩtikiumanĩte na kĩĩranĩro; no Ngai, nĩ ũndũ wa wega wake, nĩaheire Iburahĩmu igai na ũndũ wa kĩĩranĩro.
English: For if the inheritance is of the law, it is no more of promise; but God has granted it to Abraham by promise.

Kikuyu: Watho-rĩ, wakĩrĩ wa bata ũrĩkũ? Wongereirwo kũrĩ kĩĩranĩro nĩguo wonanagie mahĩtia o nginya rĩrĩa mbeũ ĩrĩa kĩrĩkanĩro kĩeranĩire ĩgooka. Watho ũcio wathanirwo nĩ araika ũkĩnengererio andũ na guoko kwa mũiguithania.
English: Then why is there the law? It was added because of transgressions, until the offspring should come to whom the promise has been made. It was ordained through angels by the hand of a mediator.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũiguithania ndakoragwo arũgamĩrĩire o mwena ũmwe; no Ngai nĩ ũmwe.
English: Now a mediator is not between one, but God is one.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, kaĩ watho ũreganĩte na ciĩranĩro cia Ngai? Aca ti ũguo! Tondũ korwo kwarĩ na watho waheanirwo nĩguo andũ magĩe na muoyo-rĩ, ti-itherũ o naguo ũthingu nĩ ũngĩakĩrehirwo nĩ watho.
English: Is the law then against the promises of God? Certainly not! For if there had been a law given which could make alive, most certainly righteousness would have been of the law.

Kikuyu: No Maandĩko moigaga atĩ andũ a thĩ nĩ mohetwo nĩ mehia, nĩgeetha kĩĩranĩro kĩrĩa kĩheanagwo nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia Jesũ Kristũ kĩheagwo o arĩa metĩkĩtie.
English: But the Scripture imprisoned all things under sin, that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro wa gwĩtĩkia ũtanooka-rĩ, twarĩ ohe nĩ watho, tũkahingĩrĩrio o nginya wĩtĩkio ũkaaguũranĩrio.
English: But before faith came, we were kept in custody under the law, confined for the faith which should afterwards be revealed.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio watho nĩguo waheirwo ũhoti wa gũtũtwara kũrĩ Kristũ, nĩguo tũtuuo athingu na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia.
English: So that the law has become our tutor to bring us to Christ, that we might be justified by faith.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kuona atĩ rĩu ũhoro wa gwĩtĩkia nĩmũkinyu, tũtingĩcooka kũrũgamĩrĩrwo nĩ watho.
English: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a tutor.

Kikuyu: Inyuothe mũrĩ ciana cia Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia Kristũ Jesũ,
English: For you are all children of God, through faith in Christ Jesus.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo inyuothe arĩa mwabatithirio thĩinĩ wa Kristũ nĩmwĩhumbĩte Kristũ.
English: For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio gũtingĩcooka gũthuuranio Mũyahudi kana Mũyunani, na gũtirĩ ngombo kana mũndũ ũtarĩ ngombo, na gũtirĩ mũndũ mũrũme kana mũndũ-wa-nja, nĩgũkorwo inyuothe mũrĩ ũmwe ki thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ.
English: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free man, there is neither male nor female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mũrĩ a Kristũ-rĩ, mũkĩrĩ a mbeũ ya Iburahĩmu, na mũrĩ agai a kũgaya kũringana na kĩĩranĩro kĩu kĩĩranĩirwo.
English: If you are Christ’s, then you are Abraham’s offspring and heirs according to promise.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa ndĩroiga nĩ atĩrĩ, ihinda rĩothe rĩrĩa mũgai arĩ o mwana-rĩ, ndarĩ ũtiganu na ngombo, o na atuĩkanĩwemweneindoiciociothe.
English: But I say that so long as the heir is a child, he is no different from a bondservant, though he is lord of all,

Kikuyu: Nĩgwathwoathagwonĩamũrorihamwenaaramatiaindociake, nginya ihinda rĩrĩa ithe atuĩte rĩgaakinya.
English: but is under guardians and stewards until the day appointed by the father.

Kikuyu: Ũguo noguo o na ithuĩ, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa twarĩ ciana, twatũire ũkombo-inĩ wa maũndũ marĩa ma ndũire ma thĩ ĩno.
English: So we also, when we were children, were held in bondage under the elemental principles of the world.

Kikuyu: No hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ihinda rĩrĩa rĩagĩrĩru kũna rĩakinyire, Ngai nĩatũmire Mũriũ, nake agĩciarwo nĩ mũndũ-wa-nja, na agĩciarwo arĩ wa gwathagwo nĩ watho,
English: But when the fullness of the time came, God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law,

Kikuyu: nĩguo akũũre andũ arĩa matũire mathagwo nĩ watho, nĩgeetha tũgĩe na kĩhooto gĩa gũtuĩka ciana ciake,
English: that he might redeem those who were under the law, that we might receive the adoption as children.

Kikuyu: Tondũ inyuĩ mũrĩ ciana ciake-rĩ, Ngai nĩatũmire Roho wa Mũriũ ngoro-inĩ ciitũ, o we Roho ũrĩa wĩtanaga ũkiugaga atĩrĩ, “Abba, Baba.”
English: And because you are children, God sent out the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, “Abba,† Father!”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, we ndũrĩ ngombo rĩngĩ, no ũrĩ mwana wake; na tondũ rĩu ũrĩ mwana wake, Ngai nĩagũtuĩte mũgai wa indo ciake.
English: So you are no longer a bondservant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.

Kikuyu: Tene rĩrĩa mũtooĩ Ngai-rĩ, mwarĩ ngombo cia ciũmbe iria itarĩ ngai.
English: However at that time, not knowing God, you were in bondage to those who by nature are not gods.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, tondũ rĩu nĩmũmenyete Ngai, kana njuge nĩmũrĩkĩtie kũmenywo nĩ Ngai-rĩ, mũrakĩenda gũcookerera maũndũ macio maagĩte hinya na matarĩ kĩene nĩkĩ? Kaĩ mũrenda gũcooka ũkombo-inĩ wamo o rĩngĩ?
English: But now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, why do you turn back again to the weak and miserable elemental principles, to which you desire to be in bondage all over again?

Kikuyu: Nĩmũrũmĩtie ũhoro wa mĩthenya mĩamũre, na mĩeri mĩamũre, na imera, o na mĩaka!
English: You observe days, months, seasons, and years.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ na kĩeha mũno tondũ gũkuoneka atĩ wĩra ũrĩa wothe ndanenogia naguo nĩ ũndũ wanyu nĩ wa tũhũ!
English: I am afraid for you, that I might have wasted my labor for you.

Kikuyu: Ndamũthaitha, inyuĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, tuĩkai ta niĩ, nĩgũkorwo niĩ nduĩkĩte ta inyuĩ. Mũtirĩ mwanjĩka ũũru.
English: I beg you, brothers, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You did me no wrong,

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa mũũĩ, ndamũhunjĩirie Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega ihinda rĩa mbere tondũ ndaarĩ mũrũaru.
English: but you know that because of weakness in the flesh I preached the Good News to you the first time.

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka ndwari yakwa yarĩ magerio kũrĩ inyuĩ, mũtianyararire kana mũkĩĩmena, no mwanyiitire ũgeni ta ndaarĩ mũraika wa Ngai, o na ta ndaarĩ Jesũ Kristũ we mwene.
English: That which was a temptation to you in my flesh, you didn’t despise nor reject; but you received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ ũndũ ũcio watũmaga mũkene ũthiĩte kũ? Niĩ ndĩ mũira atĩ, korwo nĩmũngĩahotire, nĩmũngĩakũũrire maitho manyu mũũhe.
English: What was the blessing you enjoyed? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have plucked out your eyes and given them to me.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ rĩu nduĩkĩte thũ yanyu nĩ ũndũ wa kũmũhe ũhoro ũrĩa wa ma?
English: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?

Kikuyu: Andũ acio me na kĩyo magĩcaria ũrĩa mũngĩtuĩka a thiritũ yao, no ti ũndũ wa gĩtũmi kĩega. Ũrĩa mendaga nĩ kũmũtigithania na ithuĩ mũtuĩke ao, nĩgeetha mũgĩe na kĩyo gĩa kũmarũmĩrĩra.
English: They zealously seek you in no good way. No, they desire to alienate you, that you may seek them.

Kikuyu: Nĩ wega kũgĩa na kĩyo, angĩkorwo muoroto nĩ mwega, na mwĩkage ũguo hĩndĩ ciothe to hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndĩ hamwe na inyuĩ.
English: But it is always good to be zealous in a good cause, and not only when I am present with you.

Kikuyu: Ciana ciakwa nyendete mũno, o inyuĩ arĩa njiguagĩra ruo ta rwa mũtumia akĩheo mwana, nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Kristũ agaakorwo eyũmbĩte thĩinĩ wanyu,
English: My little children, of whom I am again in travail until Christ is formed in you—

Kikuyu: naarĩ korwo ndakorwo hamwe na inyuĩ ta rĩu, nĩguo ngarũrĩre mwarĩrie wakwa tondũ nĩndigĩtwo nĩ ũhoro wanyu!
English: but I could wish to be present with you now, and to change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.

Kikuyu: Ta njĩĩrai, inyuĩ arĩa mũrenda gũkorwo mũgĩathwo nĩ watho-rĩ, kaĩ mũtooĩ ũrĩa watho uugaga?
English: Tell me, you that desire to be under the law, don’t you listen to the law?

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩ, Iburahĩmu aarĩ na ariũ eerĩ, ũmwe aaciarĩtwo nĩ mũtumia ũrĩa warĩ ngombo, nake ũcio ũngĩ agaciarwo nĩ mũtumia ũrĩa ũtaarĩ ngombo.
English: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the servant, and one by the free woman.

Kikuyu: Mũriũ wake wa mũtumia ũrĩa warĩ ngombo aaciarĩtwo na njĩra ya ũndũire; no mũriũ ũrĩa waciarĩtwo nĩ mũtumia ũcio ũtaarĩ ngombo aaciarĩtwo na ũndũ wa kũhingia kĩĩranĩro.
English: However, the son by the servant was born according to the flesh, but the son by the free woman was born through promise.

Kikuyu: Namo maũndũ macio nĩ ma ngerekano, nĩ ũndũ atumia acio marũgamĩrĩire irĩkanĩro igĩrĩ. Kĩrĩkanĩro kĩmwe nĩ gĩa Kĩrĩma gĩa Sinai nakĩo gĩciaraga ciana cia ũkombo: Nakĩo kĩgerekanĩtio na Hagari.
English: These things contain an allegory, for these are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children to bondage, which is Hagar.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Hagari arũgamĩrĩire Kĩrĩma gĩa Sinai kĩrĩa kĩrĩ kũu bũrũri wa Arabia, nake nĩwe ũgerekanĩtio na itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu ũrĩa rĩhaana ũmũthĩ, tondũ nĩatũire ũkombo-inĩ hamwe na ciana ciake.
English: For this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and answers to the Jerusalem that exists now, for she is in bondage with her children.

Kikuyu: No Jerusalemu, itũũra rĩrĩa rĩa kũũrĩa igũrũ, rĩtirĩ ũkombo-inĩ, na nĩrĩo nyina witũ,
English: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, which is the mother of us all.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Kena wee mũndũ-wa-nja thata, wee ũtaciaraga ciana, tumũra kanua na wanĩrĩre, wee ũtarĩ waigua ruo rwa kũgĩa mwana; tondũ ciana cia mũndũ-wa-nja ũrĩa ũtiganĩirio nĩ nyingĩ gũkĩra cia ũrĩa ũrĩ na mũthuuri.”
English: For it is written, “Rejoice, you barren who don’t bear. Break out and shout, you who don’t travail. For the desolate women have more children than her who has a husband.”*

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, inyuĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, mũrĩ ciana cia kĩĩranĩro, o ta ũrĩa Isaaka aarĩ.
English: Now we, brothers, as Isaac was, are children of promise.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo, mũriũ ũrĩa waciarĩtwo na njĩra ya ndũire-rĩ, nĩanyariiraga mũriũ ũcio waciarĩtwo na ũndũ wa hinya wa Roho. Ũguo noguo kũhaana o na rĩu.
English: But as then, he who was born according to the flesh persecuted him who was born according to the Spirit, so also it is now.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Maandĩko moigĩte atĩa? Moigĩte atĩrĩ, “Ingata ngombo ĩyo ya mũndũ-wa-nja na mũriũ wake, tondũ mũriũ ũcio wa mũtumia ngombo ndarĩ hĩndĩ akagayana igai hamwe na mũriũ wa mũtumia ũcio ũtarĩ ngombo.”
English: However, what does the Scripture say? “Throw out the servant and her son, for the son of the servant will not inherit with the son of the free woman.”*

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ithuĩ tũtirĩ ciana cia mũtumia ũcio ngombo, no tũrĩ ciana cia mũtumia ũcio ũtarĩ ngombo.
English: So then, brothers, we are not children of a servant, but of the free woman.

Kikuyu: Kristũ nĩgũtuohora aatuohorire nĩguo tũgĩe na wĩyathi. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mwĩhaandei wega na mũtigetĩkĩre kuohwo icooki rĩa ũkombo rĩngĩ.
English: Stand firm therefore in the liberty by which Christ has made us free, and don’t be entangled again with a yoke of bondage.

Kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩriai ciugo ciakwa! Niĩ Paũlũ ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, mũngĩtĩkĩra kũrua-rĩ, Kristũ ndangĩgĩa bata na inyuĩ o na hanini.
English: Behold, I, Paul, tell you that if you receive circumcision, Christ will profit you nothing.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩngũmwĩra o rĩngĩ atĩ mũndũ o wothe ũngĩtĩkĩra kũrua, ũcio amenye atĩ nĩagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwathĩkĩra watho ũrĩ wothe.
English: Yes, I testify again to every man who receives circumcision that he is a debtor to do the whole law.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ arĩa mũrageria gũtuĩka athingu nĩ ũndũ wa watho-rĩ, nĩmũtigithanĩtio na Kristũ; inyuĩ nĩmũgwĩte mũgatigana na wega wa Ngai.
English: You are alienated from Christ, you who desire to be justified by the law. You have fallen away from grace.

Kikuyu: No ithuĩ-rĩ, tũkoragwo twĩhokete atĩ nĩtũgatuuo athingu. Ũndũ ũcio nĩguo tũkoragwo twĩrĩgĩrĩire tũrĩ na wĩtĩkio, tũhotithĩtio nĩ Roho.
English: For we through the Spirit, by faith wait for the hope of righteousness.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ, kũrua kana kwaga kũrua gũtirĩ bata. Ũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ bata nĩ wĩtĩkio ũrĩa ũrutaga wĩra na njĩra ya wendani.
English: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision amounts to anything, but faith working through love.

Kikuyu: Nĩmũratengʼagĩra ihenya rĩega. Nũũ wamũgiririe mũgĩtiga gũcooka gwathĩkĩra ũhoro-ũrĩa- wa-ma?
English: You were running well! Who interfered with you that you should not obey the truth?

Kikuyu: Kũringĩrĩrio kwa mũthemba ũcio gũtiumĩte kũrĩ ũrĩa wamwĩtire.
English: This persuasion is not from him who calls you.

Kikuyu: “Ndawa nini tu ya kũimbia mĩgate nĩĩtũmaga mũtu ũrĩa mũkande nayo wothe ũimbe.”
English: A little yeast grows through the whole lump.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩhokete thĩinĩ wa Mwathani atĩ inyuĩ mũtikũgarũrũka mũgĩe na meciiria mangĩ. Ũrĩa ũramũtururia nĩwe ũkaaherithio o na angĩkorwo arĩ ũ.
English: I have confidence toward you in the Lord that you will think no other way. But he who troubles you will bear his judgment, whoever he is.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, angĩkorwo no ndĩrahunjia ũhoro wa kũrua-rĩ, ndĩrakĩnyariirwo nĩkĩ? Angĩkorwo ũguo nĩguo-rĩ, ũndũ ũrĩa ũtũmaga andũ mahĩngwo, na nĩguo ũhoro wa mũtharaba-rĩ, nĩmweherie.
English: But I, brothers, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still persecuted? Then the stumbling block of the cross has been removed.

Kikuyu: No nyende andũ acio mamũthĩĩnagia mathiĩ na mbere o nginya mehakũre.
English: I wish that those who disturb you would cut themselves off.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, mwetirwo nĩguo mũgĩe na wĩyathi. No rĩrĩ, mũtikahũthĩre wĩyathi wanyu kũrũmagĩrĩra merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ, no handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, tungatanagĩrai inyuĩ-ene mũrĩ na wendani.
English: For you, brothers, were called for freedom. Only don’t use your freedom as an opportunity for the flesh, but through love be servants to one another.

Kikuyu: Tondũ-rĩ, ũhoro wothe wa watho ũhingagio nĩ rĩathani rĩĩrĩ rĩmwe: “Enda mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ o ta ũrĩa wĩendete wee mwene.”
English: For the whole law is fulfilled in one word, in this: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”*

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũngĩrũmana mũkĩrĩĩanaga-rĩ, mwĩmenyererei mũtikaniinane inyuĩ ene.
English: But if you bite and devour one another, be careful that you don’t consume one another.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nguuga atĩrĩ, thiiagai mũtongoretio nĩ Roho, na mũtirĩhingagia merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ.
English: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you won’t fulfill the lust of the flesh.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mwĩrĩ nĩwĩriragĩria maũndũ marĩa matangĩendwo nĩ Roho, nake Roho nĩareganaga na maũndũ marĩa meriragĩrio nĩ mwĩrĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Roho na mwĩrĩ itũũraga irũaga, na nĩkĩo mũtahotaga gwĩka ũrĩa mwendaga gwĩka.
English: For the flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; and these are contrary to one another, that you may not do the things that you desire.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũngĩtongorio nĩ Roho, inyuĩ mũtingĩkorwo mũgĩathwo nĩ watho.
English: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, maũndũ marĩa mekagwo nĩ mwĩrĩ nĩmoĩo, namo nĩ: ũmaraya, na meciiria mooru, na ũmaramari,
English: Now the deeds of the flesh are obvious, which are: adultery, sexual immorality, uncleanness, lustfulness,

Kikuyu: na kũhooya mĩhianano, na kũrogana, na ũthũ, na kũũgitana, na ũiru, na mangʼũrĩ, na kwĩyenda, na nyamũkano, na ngarari,
English: idolatry, sorcery, hatred, strife, jealousies, outbursts of anger, rivalries, divisions, heresies,

Kikuyu: gũcumĩkĩra na kũrĩĩo nĩ njoohi, na maruga marĩa mainagwo nyĩmbo iria itaagĩrĩire, o na maũndũ mangĩ ta macio. Nĩndamũkaanirie, o ta ũrĩa ndamũkaanirie hĩndĩ ya mbere, atĩ andũ arĩa mekaga maũndũ ta macio matikagaya ũthamaki wa Ngai.
English: envy, murders, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these; of which I forewarn you, even as I also forewarned you, that those who practice such things will not inherit God’s Kingdom.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, maciaro ma Roho nĩ wendani, na gĩkeno, na thayũ, na gũkirĩrĩria, na kũiguanĩra tha, na gũtugana, na kwĩhokeka,
English: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith,†

Kikuyu: na ũhooreri, na kwĩgirĩrĩria merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ. Macio-rĩ, gũtirĩ watho wa kũgiria mekagwo.
English: gentleness, and self-control. Against such things there is no law.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ arĩa marĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ nĩmambithĩtie mĩĩrĩ yao mũtĩ-igũrũ hamwe na thuti ciayo na merirĩria mayo.
English: Those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh with its passions and lusts.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo tũtũire muoyo nĩ ũndũ wa Roho-rĩ, kĩrekei tũtongoragio nĩ Roho.
English: If we live by the Spirit, let’s also walk by the Spirit.

Kikuyu: Rekei tũtige gwĩtĩĩa, na kũũgitana, o na kũiguanagĩra ũiru.
English: Let’s not become conceited, provoking one another, and envying one another.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, mũndũ angĩkorwo ahĩtĩtie-rĩ, inyuĩ arĩa mũrĩ na roho mũcookagĩrĩriei mũhooreire. No mwĩmenyererei, o na inyuĩ mũtikae kũgerio.
English: Brothers, even if a man is caught in some fault, you who are spiritual must restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness, looking to yourself so that you also aren’t tempted.

Kikuyu: Teithanagiai mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ maũndũ marĩa mamũritũhagĩra, na mweka ũguo-rĩ, nĩmũrĩhingagia watho wa Kristũ.
English: Bear one another’s burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩĩciiria atĩ we nĩ wa bata hĩndĩ ĩrĩa atarĩ kĩene-rĩ, nĩ kwĩheenia areheenia.
English: For if a man thinks himself to be something when he is nothing, he deceives himself.

Kikuyu: O mũndũ nĩarorage wĩra wake mwene. Hĩndĩ ĩyo no ahote kwĩraha, atekwĩiganania na mũndũ ũngĩ,
English: But let each man examine his own work, and then he will have reason to boast in himself, and not in someone else.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo o mũndũ no nginya akuue mũrigo wake.
English: For each man will bear his own burden.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũrutagwo ũhoro wa Ngai nĩagayanage indo ciothe iria njega na mũmũruti.
English: But let him who is taught in the word share all good things with him who teaches.

Kikuyu: Mũtikanaheenio: Ngai ndangĩnyũrũrio. Kĩrĩa mũndũ ahaandaga no kĩo agethaga.
English: Don’t be deceived. God is not mocked, for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhaandaga maũndũ ma gũkenia merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ-rĩ, ũcio akaagetha kwanangĩka kũrĩa kuumanaga na maũndũ macio. No ũrĩa ũhaandaga maũndũ ma gũkenia Roho-rĩ, ũcio akaagetha muoyo wa tene na tene ũrĩa uumanaga na Roho.
English: For he who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption. But he who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ tũtikanogio nĩ gwĩka wega, nĩgũkorwo ihinda rĩrĩa rĩagĩrĩru rĩakinya nĩtũkaagetha magetha twaga gũkua ngoro.
English: Let’s not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season if we don’t give up.

Kikuyu: Na nĩ ũndũ ũcio, rĩrĩa rĩothe twagĩa na ihinda rĩagĩrĩru, nĩtwĩkage andũ othe maũndũ mega, na makĩria twĩkage wega andũ arĩa marĩ a nyũmba ya arĩa metĩkĩtie.
English: So then, as we have opportunity, let’s do what is good toward all men, and especially toward those who are of the household of the faith.

Kikuyu: Onei ũrĩa ndĩramwandĩkĩra marũa maya na ndemwa nene na guoko gwakwa mwene!
English: See with what large letters I write to you with my own hand.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio mendaga kwĩonania atĩ nĩagĩrĩru makĩonwo-rĩ, nĩo maramũringĩrĩria atĩ mũrue. Mekaga ũguo no geetha matikanyariirwo nĩ ũndũ wa mũtharaba wa Kristũ.
English: As many as desire to make a good impression in the flesh compel you to be circumcised, just so they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.

Kikuyu: O na acio maruaga matiathĩkagĩra watho, no nĩmendaga inyuĩ mũrue nĩguo metĩĩage nĩ ũndũ wa mĩĩrĩ yanyu.
English: For even they who receive circumcision don’t keep the law themselves, but they desire to have you circumcised, so that they may boast in your flesh.

Kikuyu: No niĩ-rĩ, ndĩroaga gwĩtĩĩa, tiga o ndĩtĩĩire nĩ ũndũ wa mũtharaba wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ, na nĩ ũndũ waguo-rĩ, maũndũ ma thĩ nĩmambĩtwo mũtĩ-igũrũ nĩguo mehere harĩ niĩ, na niĩ ngaambwo harĩ maũndũ ma thĩ nĩguo njehere harĩ mo.
English: But far be it from me to boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.

Kikuyu: Kũrua kana kwaga kũrua gũtirĩ bata; kĩrĩa kĩrĩ bata nĩ mũndũ agarũrũke atuĩke kĩũmbe kĩerũ.
English: For in Christ Jesus neither is circumcision anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.

Kikuyu: Thayũ na kũiguĩrwo tha kũrogĩa na andũ arĩa marũmagĩrĩra wathani ũyũ, o hamwe na andũ a Isiraeli arĩa a Ngai.
English: As many as walk by this rule, peace and mercy be on them, and on God’s Israel.

Kikuyu: Ngĩrĩkĩrĩria nguuga atĩrĩ, gũtikagĩe mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ũkũndehere thĩĩna, nĩgũkorwo nĩnjoretwo njoro cia Jesũ mwĩrĩ-inĩ wakwa.
English: From now on, let no one cause me any trouble, for I bear the marks of the Lord Jesus branded on my body.

Kikuyu: Wega wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ ũrogĩa na maroho manyu, inyuĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ. Ameni.
English: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.
